response.global_summary: The video features a live concert-style performance by three virtual performers. The performance consists of two distinct songs: 'Gunjo Sanka' by Eve, followed by 'Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari' by kemu. The stage setup includes dynamic lighting, geometric background designs, and animated visual displays.

scenes.description: The virtual performers are on a stage with a futuristic design, singing 'Gunjo Sanka'. They are wearing coordinated outfits and performing choreographed dance moves. The stage features colorful lights and balloons in the background.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
You said you want to stay connected; I can believe those words
But we’ve already said goodbye to the us of yesterday
The blue spring has passed, now it’s distant lightning
If I’d started crying and just looked forward
I’d have probably felt a lot more at ease
A sliver of hope shook my heart
I stopped asking for the impossible, I’m an incomplete human
Only this wonderful world holds the answers
There are many regrets waiting for you up ahead,
So many that you might give up.
But no more, you won’t hesitate anymore
Your scars will pile up until you can think of them fondly
It’s ok to be clumsy, so
Run, run at your own pace, let your voice roar
Embrace your hopes and worries equally
Illuminate the future that connects you and the present
We don’t know names or faces
We just strum the same note
It’s a moment like a miracle that seems like a prank
Time passes by in the blink of an eye
I’m afraid of staying as I am
But I can’t turn back the time I’ve lost.
If you keep looking back at the past, then it’s ok to cry
Because bitterness and pain will only affirm you
When we become adults, we forget.
The place where we made our promise
Is already gone. I want to remember it.
Our memories will pile up until we can think of them fondly
It’s ok to be clumsy, so
Let your voice echo far and wide
I’ve come this far for a definite reason
My journey hasn’t ended
Before I knew it, my feelings took shape. This morning steeped in pure white,
I still can’t forget it, so…
There are many things up ahead, so many that you might give up
But no more, you won’t hesitate anymore
The things that are irreplaceable to you are overflowing
The answer is in your hands
Don’t let go, your story is taking another step forward
There are many regrets waiting for you up ahead
So many that you might give up
But no more, you won’t hesitate anymore
Your scars will pile up until you can think of them fondly
It’s ok to be clumsy, so
Run, run at your own pace, let your voice roar
Embrace your hopes and worries equally
Illuminate the future that connects you and the present

scenes.description: The stage lighting shifts to a more dramatic, darker tone. One of the performers stands alone on stage singing 'Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari', later joined by the other two. Visuals on stage change to match the song's more intense mood.

scenes.reference_lyrics_en:
A tale of a trillion years and one night
In a village in an unknown era
A young boy, unknown
A fairy tale that no one knows
Since the time I was born
Treated like an unwanted demon child
I received
An unjust punishment
There is no reason to be sad, but
Beyond the sunset, I am led by the hand
I don’t know, I don’t know, I don’t know anything
Not the kindness after the reprimand
Or the warmth of hands after the rain
But it is really, really, really, really cold
I don’t die, I don’t die, why don’t I die?
Even though I can’t even have one dream
This fairy tale that no one knows
Was absorbed by the sunset
And disappeared
A violence almost spat out
Looks of contempt daily
Suddenly
You were there
Even though I shouldn’t speak to you
“I want to know your name”
Sorry, I don’t have a name
Or a tongue
Although there is no place for me anywhere
“Let’s go home together,” led by the hand
I don’t know, I don’t know, I don’t know anything
Not that you are not a child anymore
The warmth of a stranger’s hand
Is really, really, really just the truth
You don’t stop, you don’t stop, why don’t you stop?
Even though you will be killed if found
Two unwanted children after the rain
Were absorbed by the sunset
And disappeared
The day ends, the night ends
We played until tired and hugged each other
In this world, other than you and me
It would be good if there was no one
It would be good if there was no one
I don’t know, I don’t know, a voice can be heard
It’s not mine, nor yours, but all of humanity
Without resisting, we were led by the hand
And were absorbed by the sunset
And disappeared
I don’t know, I don’t know, I don’t know anything
Not what comes now, nor your name
For now, for now, this is fine
It’s just what I think, really, really, really, really
I don’t know, I don’t know, that ringing
Was absorbed by the sunset and disappeared

response.thoughts:
**My Analysis of the Virtual Performers Video**

Okay, so I need to break down this video and extract some information. My goal is to identify the songs being performed by these virtual artists and then pull their official lyrics, both in Japanese and English translations. Here's my thought process:

First, I need to analyze the video itself. It's got three virtual performers, dancing and singing. At the very beginning, and this is crucial, the on-screen text shows "群青讃歌" (Gunjo Sanka). This goes on until about the 4:18 mark. Then, at 4:19, the scene changes, and the text now displays "六兆年と一夜物語" (Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari) until the end of the video.

So, I'm going to create a scene mapping: Scene 1 covers 0:00 to 4:18, featuring "群青讃歌." Scene 2 is from 4:19 to the end at 5:00, containing "六兆年と一夜物語."

Next, I need to gather the song metadata. For "群青讃歌," I know the title is "群青讃歌" (Gunjo Sanka), the original artist or composer is Eve, and it's being performed by a group of three. For the search, I probably don't need the specific performers, just the title and composer. The language is Japanese. For "六兆年と一夜物語," the title is "六兆年と一夜物語" (Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari), the original artist or composer is kemu, and the language is, again, Japanese.

Now, I'll formulate my search strategy. I need to find the official lyrics in Japanese and English translations. I'm going to use parallel searches to get these quickly. My search queries will be:

*   "群青讃歌 Eve 歌詞" (Japanese lyrics for Song 1)
*   "Gunjo Sanka Eve lyrics English" (English lyrics for Song 1)
*   "六兆年と一夜物語 kemu 歌詞" (Japanese lyrics for Song 2)
*   "Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari kemu lyrics English" (English lyrics for Song 2)

I'll execute this using the `web_search_langsearch_multi` tool. After I get the results back, I'll analyze them to confirm they are the official lyrics and populate my data structure, which I'll then format into the `final_result` JSON object.



**My Analysis of Lyric Data**

Okay, I've got the raw lyric data for both "群青讃歌" (Gunjo Sanka) by Eve and "六兆年と一夜物語" (Rokuchounen to Ichiya Monogatari) by kemu. My primary goal, as always, is to ensure the integrity of the information. I need to deliver the full, complete lyrics without truncation, as specified in the instructions.

I've examined the search results meticulously. For "群青讃歌," I found a couple of strong candidates for the full lyrics at both `https://www.susanguitar.com/geci/1617752` and `https://www.juzikong.com/works/70a6686e-cc86-4a4a-8dff-bbaaae25d608` although I need to check them to be completely certain. The other results provide lyrics plus translations, which might be helpful for context, but I only need the original.

Similarly, for "六兆年と一夜物語," there are several options. I've noted a potentially good source at `https://www.doc88.com/p-9089005739589.html`, and a full lyric plus translation at `http://www.jitashe.org/tab/87103/`. The first result appears to be incomplete.

I'm confident I have enough data points to construct the `final_result` accurately. My next step will be to verify the lyrics from the sources mentioned above, and then I will structure the response accordingly. I'm ready to proceed with the final response.
